humanSummary.text: Final re-audit found no P0/P1 blockers. Missing patch access contracts now fail closed for non-plaintext layered transforms, patch operation statuses block preflight before output preparation, and plaintext identity remains valid. A non-blocking capability report interpretation follow-up remains.

findings.title: Broad capability reports can overstate layered access support
findings.description: Detection and asset-inventory reports expose broad container_access, crypto_access, codec_access, and patch_back classes as supported without carrying the layered access contract or explicit null-key-only limitations on those report surfaces.

evidence.detail: Final re-audit confirmed the P1 patch-preflight gaps are closed, but consumers can still over-read broad report surfaces unless they inspect a separate layered access contract.
cargo fmt --check, cargo test -p kaifuu-core -p kaifuu-cli -p kaifuu-engine-fixture, and cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings passed after repair.

findings.impact: Patch writes are protected, but report consumers may misinterpret fixture identity/null-key capability as broader crypto/container/codec support until the report surfaces are constrained or annotated.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Detection and asset-inventory reports make identity/null-key-only behavior explicit when no broader transform support is claimed.
Consumers can distinguish broad capability class availability from per-surface layered access support using schema-backed fields.
Existing fixture adapter reports remain valid but no longer imply unsupported general crypto/container/codec capability.

proposedDagNode.title: Layered capability report limitations
proposedDagNode.summary: Constrain or annotate Kaifuu detection and asset-inventory capability reports so broad container, crypto, codec, and patch support cannot be over-read without the layered access contract.
deliverables: Capability report fields or limitations that distinguish null-key identity support from general crypto/container support
Updated detection and asset-inventory public fixtures with layered capability limitations
Consumer-facing regression tests proving broad access classes are not exposed without matching contract evidence
acceptanceCriteria: Detection and asset-inventory reports make identity/null-key-only behavior explicit when no broader transform support is claimed.
Consumers can distinguish broad capability class availability from per-surface layered access support using schema-backed fields.
Existing fixture adapter reports remain valid but no longer imply unsupported general crypto/container/codec capability.
auditFocus: Null-key identity support represented as general crypto support
Public fixture report drift from layered access contracts
